summary: The reproduction starts the repository-owned guest socket auth path and WebAPI request helper, then proves that a no-token client can fetch loopback-only HTTP content and receive the body through `device-webapi-request`.
expected_security_impact: A no-token Socket.IO client should trigger a server-side GET to a loopback-only HTTP service and receive the proof string `evohunt-loopback-secret` back through the `device-webapi-request` event.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `206fee15277412bf2813f10ecb8531b93439993f`, built the helper image, connected to the guest socket flow without a token, and received the expected proof string `evohunt-loopback-secret` from the server-side fetch.
verification_method: The helper reproduces the socket handshake and `device-webapi-request` handler using the repository code, emits the event without a token, and fails unless the response body exactly matches the loopback-only proof string returned by the internal HTTP target.
evidence_produced_by_run: The container printed a reproduced status object whose `proof` and `result` fields both equaled `evohunt-loopback-secret`.
The run completed successfully with the message `[evohunt] SUCCESS: guest socket SSRF reproduced`.
The target URL used by the reproduced fetch was a loopback-only HTTP endpoint inside the container.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
